feed.title: Combined summary - [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero

entry.title: Combined summary - [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
entry.summary: Junhyuk Lee's research paper delves into the significant challenges Bitcoin might face regarding its security as the block rewards continue to decrease, a situation that is expected to become critical by 2140. The reduction of block rewards raises concerns about the profitability for miners, which could potentially not be offset by increases in Bitcoin prices. This shift could profoundly affect the security framework of Bitcoin when there is no more block reward. Lee introduces a concept called G_t to monitor and predict when miners might start deviating from their expected behaviors within the Bitcoin network. The study suggests that deviations in miner behavior could arise not only from changes in block rewards but also from other factors. It particularly emphasizes the risks associated in scenarios where miners rely solely on transaction fees for revenue. Under these conditions, deviations could happen with transaction fees as low as 0.17% of the transaction values, pointing to potential instability in a pure fee-only regime. In response to these findings, suggestions have been made on possible solutions to mitigate such risks. The implementation of a minimum floor fee, which is feasible through a soft fork, has been discussed alongside more comprehensive measures like a tail emission combined with an EIP-1559 style fee burn and priority tipping mechanism. These strategies aim to provide a more stable financial incentive for miners, ensuring the longevity and security of the Bitcoin network. For further discussion and perspectives on addressing the diminishing block subsidy, you can visit this [post](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/addressing-the-diminishing-block-subsidy/2640).
